era_context: Middle Bronze Age. The Hyksos rule Lower Egypt; Kassite power consolidates in Mesopotamia; Minoan Crete is at the height of its cultural and commercial influence; the Indus Valley Civilization is in its mature phase. In China, the Xia or early Shang period continues.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. No specific events are known for this year.
historiographic_note: This year has no documented specific events in any surviving historical source. The historical record for -1654 BCE is entirely absent. This is characteristic of the Bronze Age: most individual years are historically invisible unless they witnessed major events that were recorded or left substantial archaeological traces. Our knowledge of this period comes from long-term archaeological patterns, stratigraphy, and later historical syntheses rather than year-by-year documentation.
